channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Critique] New wp2shell WordPress Core Flaw Lets Unauthenticated Attackers Run Code
item.description:
An anonymous HTTP request can run code on a WordPress site. The bug is in core, so a bare install with zero plugins is exploitable.
Every 6.9 and 7.0 site was in range until Friday, when WordPress shipped 6.9.5 and…

item.title: [Faible] Abbott probes two cyber incidents amid extortion claims
item.description: Abbott Laboratories is investigating two separate cybersecurity incidents after confirming unauthorized access to internal legacy Exact Sciences systems in its Cancer Diagnostics business, while also investigating a…

item.title: [Moyen] OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
item.description:
Eleven bytes will make an unpatched OpenSSL server set aside up to 131 KB of memory for a message that never arrives. On the glibc systems Okta tested, that memory is gone until the process restarts.
OpenSSL shipped…

item.title: [Faible] Seven Malicious Vite npm Packages Use Blockchain C2 to Deliver a RAT
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a cluster of seven malicious npm packages targeting the Vite frontend tooling ecosystem as part of a software supply chain attack.
The malicious package campaign, codenamed…

item.title: [Élevé] SFR : 2,1 millions de clients fibre en fuite après l’accès à un outil interne
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à SFR a été revendiquée le 17 juillet 2026. L’auteur affirme avoir accédé... L’article SFR : 2,1 millions de clients fibre en fuite après l’accès à un outil interne est apparu en premier…

item.title: [Élevé] HollowByte DDoS flaw bloats OpenSSL server memory with 11-byte payload
item.description: A vulnerability dubbed HollowByte allows unauthenticated attackers to trigger a denial-of-service (DoS) condition on OpenSSL servers with a malicious payload of just 11 bytes. [...]

item.title: [Faible] New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens
item.description:
A Go botnet called NadMesh turned up in early July hunting exposed AI services, and the operator's own dashboard claims 3,811 unique AWS keys.
A Shodan harvester keeps the scan queue stocked with ComfyUI, Ollama, n8n,…

item.title: [Faible] GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have attributed the April 2026 DigiCert security incident to a threat activity cluster dubbed CylindricalCanine.
Expel, which shared technical details of the event, described the threat actor…

item.title: [Critique] Royaume-Uni : couvre-feu numérique pour les adolescents
item.description: Londres prépare un couvre-feu numérique et renforce le contrôle d’âge pour protéger les adolescents en ligne.

item.title: [Élevé] NEBBIA visée par une fuite de données clients
item.description: Un pro du fitness et de la musculation touché par une fuite pirate de 318 002 documents clients.

item.title: [Faible] NoName057(16) revendique un piège contre Madame X
item.description: Des pirates affirment avoir piégé une journaliste dans une opération mêlant infiltration, cybermenace et propagande.

item.title: [Élevé] Ernst & Young discloses data breach after support system hack
item.description: Ernst & Young is notifying customers of a data breach caused by the compromise of a third-party support ticket system used by its IT personnel. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] Des pirates prorusses ciblent encore la France en ligne
item.description: Pirates prorusses : caméras exposées et DDoS contre la France servent une stratégie de propagande numérique.

item.title: [Faible] Inside the Search for "Clean" Residential Proxies for Carding
item.description: Residential proxies are no longer the silver bullet they once were for carding. Flare explains why cybercriminals increasingly seek "clean" residential proxies and combine them with browser fingerprints, device…

item.title: [Faible] Fake Coding Tests Deliver OtterCookie-Aligned Malware Hidden in SVG Flag Images
item.description: North Korean threat actors linked to the Contagious Interview campaign have been observed employing steganography in SVG image files to conceal malicious payloads as part of a campaign using fake job postings and coding…

item.title: [Faible] Dairy company Fairlife suspends production in US after cyber incident
item.description: Fairlife’s U.S. operation includes plants in Michigan, New York and Arizona, and the company's retail sales passed $1 billion in 2022.

item.title: [Faible] Croqvacances : 72 916 personnes exposées ?
item.description: Un pirate aurait exposé plus de 70 000 données personnelles aprés l'exfiltration d'informations volées à Croqvacances.

item.title: [Faible] Zelensky appoints Ukraine's acting security service chief as acting defense minister
item.description: Yevhenii Khmara, a major general with deep experience in intelligence, counterterrorism and long-range strikes against Russia, is Ukraine's new acting defense minister.

item.title: [Faible] E.U. Orders Google to Open Android Mic, Camera and Screen to Rival AI Assistants
item.description: The European Commission on Thursday ordered Google to give rival AI assistants the same reach into Android that Gemini already has: the camera, the microphone, whatever is on screen, a wake word that fires with the…

item.title: [Critique] The Race to Field Military Autonomy Is On, Can Trusted Information Infrastructure Keep Pace?
item.description: Military forces are under increasing pressure to field autonomous capabilities faster than ever before. Across the U.S., UK, and NATO, new investment, evolving defense strategies, and accelerated acquisition pathways…

item.title: [Critique] New Windows LegacyHive zero-day gives hackers admin privileges
item.description: A security researcher using the "Nightmare Eclipse" handle has released a Windows zero-day exploit dubbed LegacyHive that allows attackers to escalate privileges on up-to-date Windows systems. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Armenia Detains Russian Tourist on U.S. Warrant for REvil Hacker, Lawyers Say Wrong Man
item.description:
Armenia has held a Russian tourist named Aleksandr Ermakov in a detention center since June 28, on a U.S. extradition request for a REvil ransomware suspect named Aleksandr Ermakov.
His wife, Maria Yurova, told REN TV…

item.title: [Faible] 16 millions de CV exposés en ligne
item.description: Une annonce pirate revendique 16 millions de CV, avec coordonnées, adresses et historiques professionnels.

item.title: [Faible] Windows Server 2022 reach end of mainstream support in 90 days
item.description: Microsoft announced that Windows Server 2022 will reach the mainstream end date in October 2026, but will switch to extended support and continue receiving security updates for five more years. [...]

item.title: [Faible] ACR Stealer Uses ClickFix Lures to Steal Browser Tokens and Microsoft 365 Files
item.description: ACR Stealer, an infostealer in circulation since 2024, is walking out of enterprise networks with saved browser passwords, live session tokens, PDFs, Microsoft 365 documents, and files from synced OneDrive and…

item.title: [Faible] New GoSerpent Malware Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Diplomats for Espionage
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented malware called GoSerpent that has been put to use in cyber attacks targeting entities in Southeast Asia since late 2025 with a focus on long-term…

item.title: [Faible] US charges two over laundering $43 million from investment fraud
item.description: U.S. prosecutors on Thursday charged a New York man and woman for their roles in a large-scale crime ring that laundered money stolen in cyber investment fraud scams. [...]
